The **Chinese FA Cup** ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 中国足协杯, abbreviated as **CFA Cup**) is the national knockout cup competition in China organized by the [Chinese Football Association](/wiki/Chinese_Football_Association "Chinese Football Association"). The current holders are [Beijing Guoan](/wiki/Beijing_Guoan_F.C. "Beijing Guoan F.C."), having beaten [Henan](/wiki/Henan_F.C. "Henan F.C.") in [2025](/wiki/2025_Chinese_FA_Cup "2025 Chinese FA Cup") for their sixth title.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chinese_FA_Cup&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The competition started as the Chinese National Football Championship ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 全国足球锦标赛) in 1956.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was reorganized after the [Cultural Revolution](/wiki/Cultural_Revolution "Cultural Revolution") and used the name Chinese FA Cup for the first time in 1984. It was scrapped for the 6th National Games of China in 1987, and was reorganized again as the Chinese National Cup Winners' Cup ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 全国足球优胜者杯赛) between 1990 and 1992 as the qualifiers for the [Asian Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/Asian_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "Asian Cup Winners' Cup").

The current format of the FA Cup began in the 1995 season following the establishment of the professional football league in China. The cup was temporary scrapped in 2007 due to the [Chinese Football Association](/wiki/Chinese_Football_Association "Chinese Football Association")'s strategy for the [2008 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2008_Summer_Olympics "2008 Summer Olympics"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and returned in [2011](/wiki/2011_Chinese_FA_Cup "2011 Chinese FA Cup").
